prompt: Create a clean 2x2 grid of four front-facing 3D typographic sculptures on a light gray seamless studio background, separated by thin white dividers. Each quadrant features a single giant letter built entirely from dense, realistic filmmaking equipment and on-set props from a different cinematic era, with the letterform chaotic but clearly legible. Use high-end Cinema 4D product-render aesthetics, ambient occlusion, realistic scale relationships, intricate kit-bashing, subtle wear, metallic reflections, and three-point studio lighting with a soft overhead source. Top-left: the letter S, representing early silent-era or proto-cinema filmmaking, constructed from antique brass-and-wood cameras, exposed gears, film reels, projector parts, bellows, tripod wood legs, and vintage optical housings; warm bronze and copper tones; include 3 extra props at the base: a black megaphone labeled "DIRECTOR", a clapboard, and a short stack of film canisters; one projector-like lens at the upper right glows brightly. Top-right: the letter C, representing classical Hollywood film production, built from black metal film cameras, studio lenses, magazines, matte boxes, dolly components, control boxes, and analog set hardware; mostly black, steel, and gunmetal with subtle brass accents; include 4 extra props integrated around the base and inner right side: a clapperboard, a boxy side module, a film magazine marked "35", and a small cylindrical canister; a large forward-facing lamp or lens glows at the upper right. Bottom-left: the letter N, representing modern analog or late-20th-century cinema, assembled from Arriflex-style cameras, long cine zoom lenses, support rigs, rails, cables, a slate, and a field recorder; predominantly matte black with silver hardware and a few red accent rings; include 3 clearly visible base elements: a clapperboard section, a labeled director strip, and a rectangular audio recorder/control unit at the bottom. Bottom-right: the letter D, representing digital and virtual production, built from RED-style digital cinema cameras, monitors, battery packs, wireless transmitters, drones, LED panels, green-screen fabric inserts, and modular rig components; black technical bodies with vivid green accents and screen graphics; include 4 notable integrated device types: drone arms/propellers at the top, a rectangular LED light panel, multiple monitors, and a large camera lens at the lower left. Keep all objects grounded and physically connected, no floating pieces, no flat typography, no handwriting, no paper look, no messy background. Ultra-detailed, sharp, 8k, polished graphic design asset for a poster or visual comparison of filmmaking eras.
